name: Maristan
theoryOfChange: Maristan describes an approach grounded in Islamic traditions that integrates professional clinical care, education, and research to advance holistic mental and spiritual wellness; it aims to bridge research with practice (as community partner of the Stanford Muslim Mental Health & Islamic Psychology Lab) and to equip religious, community, and clinical leaders through evidence-based training to address mental health crises within Muslim communities.
scoreSummary: Too early to rate numerically. This organization was founded in 2022 and is shown as an emerging organization while it builds a public track record.

evaluationSources: UC San Diego Center for Community Health
Stanford University (Muslim Mental Health & Islamic Psychology Lab)
Community Partner (Stanford University Muslim Mental Health & Islamic Psychology Lab)

zakatClaimEvidence: Help heal minds and hearts of our Ummah by supporting Maristan's essential, faith-centered mental health programs: Zakat-Eligible Mental Health Clinic, Healing & Learning Circle, Mental Health Trainings & Psychoeducation using evidence-based information to build mental health literacy and reduce stigma. 100% of your Zakat will go directly to Zakat-eligible clients receiving clinical therapy. (Source: https://amwkgvxs.donorsupport.co/page/FUNBFXDFWXS)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Early-stage organization. We show qualitative context while it builds a longer public track record.

Maristan operates programs including the Maristan Clinic and Education and Training.
The organization provides clinical mental health services and professional training.

dimension_explanations.alignment: Maristan shows high donor fit for those seeking to support global health and mental health within a Muslim context, as it maintains a clear zakat policy [2]. The organization demonstrates strong fundraising efficiency, spending only $0.11 per $1 raised [1].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization focuses on capacity building through its Maristan Clinic and professional education programs [4][5]. While it maintains a healthy $658,402 in total revenue and 13.3 months of working capital, specific cost-per-beneficiary data is currently unavailable [1][3].

strengths.detail: The organization maintains <cite id="7">13.3 months of working capital</cite>, providing significant resilience for an early-stage nonprofit.
Maristan spends only <cite id="6">$0.11 per $1 raised</cite>, well below the industry average for emerging health organizations.
Donations are <cite id="5">zakat-eligible</cite>, specifically serving the asnaf categories of the poor and needy.

strengths_deep_dive: Maristan's primary strength lies in its specialized clinical model. The organization provides <cite id="4">Healing Circles and Suicide Response Training</cite> that are specifically designed for Muslim populations. This cultural competency is backed by a <cite id="8">documented theory of change</cite> that integrates faith-based healing with modern psychiatry. Financially, the charity has shown <cite id="9">early-stage growth</cite>, with revenue increasing from $129,957 in 2022 to over $658,000 by 2024. This trajectory suggests a strong community demand for their specialized mental health services.
rich_narrative.summary: Maristan is an early-stage community mental health organization that bridges the gap between Islamic principles and clinical care. The organization focuses on providing <cite id="1">culturally sensitive clinical care and professional education</cite> to address the unique mental health needs of the Muslim community. Since receiving its <cite id="2">501(c)(3) status in March 2022</cite>, Maristan has rapidly expanded its reach. The charity reported <cite id="3">$618,632 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 tax year, representing significant growth from its founding. Their service model is diverse, including the <cite id="4">Maristan Clinic, Healing Circles, and Suicide Response Certification Training</cite>. By integrating spiritual wellness with psychological science, the organization provides a specialized alternative to mainstream mental health services. Maristan maintains a <cite id="5">zakat-eligible status</cite>, allowing Muslim donors to fulfill their religious obligations while supporting mental health initiatives. The organization operates with high financial efficiency, spending only <cite id="6">$0.11 to raise every dollar</cite>. This lean fundraising approach ensures that a significant portion of contributions is available for program development. Maristan also maintains strong financial stability, holding <cite id="7">13.3 months of working capital</cite> in reserve. This provides a solid buffer for an emerging institution as it scales its clinical and research operations.
